commit.message:
perf: v2_scalars first-round optimization in Dory opening (#10) (#17)

In round 0 of reduce-and-fold, v2_work[i] = g2_fin * v_vec[i], so
the multi-pairing Σ e(g1[i], g2_fin * v[i]) simplifies to
e(MSM(g1, v), g2_fin). This replaces 2×n2 Miller loops with 2 G1
MSMs + 2 single pairings, matching upstream Jolt's v2_scalars
optimization. Applied to both symmetric and asymmetric opening paths.

All 8 test programs verified against upstream Jolt verifier.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: zolt
repository.description: Jolt-compatible zkVM prover in pure Zig with zero dependencies, zero FFI

commit.message:
perf: parallel polynomial commits — all 40 at once (#18)

* wip: parallel polynomial commits (#12)

* perf: parallel polynomial commits via parallelForEach (#12)

Add ThreadPool.parallelForEach for fine-grained work distribution
(chunk_size=1) and use it to run all ~40 Dory polynomial commits in
parallel. Build witness data upfront (Phase A), then dispatch all
commits concurrently (Phase B) with nested work-stealing via
waitAndWork. ~15% speedup on primes_large (15.7s → 13.4s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify parallelForEach and document OOM assumption

Remove redundant Helper wrapper in parallelForEach — pass func directly
to spawnWg. Add comments clarifying that catch blocks only handle OOM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden OAuth 2.0: fix PKCE bypass, open redirect, token leakage + 5 high-severity issues

Critical fixes:
- PKCE code_verifier now mandatory when code_challenge stored (was skippable)
- redirect_uri validated against registered client at authorize + token endpoints
- Per-session access tokens replace raw MCP_API_KEY exposure

High fixes:
- Rate limit /register (5/min), /token (10/min), /authorize (10/min)
- Size caps + TTL cleanup on all in-memory OAuth dicts
- CORS restricted from wildcard to claude.ai/anthropic.com origins
- Auth code bound to client_id at /token time
- CSRF token on OAuth consent form (10-min TTL)

ci: fix Leptonica .pc filename on Windows cross-compile

CMake's file(GENERATE) appends the build configuration suffix
(e.g. _Release) to the .pc filename when using a toolchain file.
This produces lept_Release.pc instead of lept.pc, breaking the
Requires.private chain from tesseract.pc.

Rename any lept_*.pc to lept.pc after install.

commit.message:
ci: move -lws2_32 to --extra-libs for correct Windows link order

Same link-order issue as the C++ runtime: -lws2_32 in --extra-ldflags
(maps to $LDFLAGS) appears before object files and static archives in
the link command. Move it to --extra-libs so it appears after
libtesseract.a, allowing ws2_32 symbols to resolve.

then syncs with Firestore in background.

fix: enable on-the-fly package installation in sandbox test runners

Exercises requiring packages beyond what's pre-installed (e.g. TanStack
React Query, requests) were failing because:

1. deps.json/requirements.txt in supportFiles landed in /workspace/support/
   but test runners only searched /workspace/ — added SUPPORT_DIR to lookup
2. Node.js runners ran npm install with cwd /workspace, which destroyed
   the /workspace/node_modules symlink and wiped all pre-installed packages
   (react, vitest, testing-library, etc.) — changed cwd to /opt/sandbox
3. Updated prompt builder to instruct the LLM how to include dependency
   files in supportFiles for exercises that need external packages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove useScrollReveal from /platform — root cause of invisible tab content

useScrollReveal observes .reveal elements once on mount. Feature cards
rendered on tab switch are created after the observer ran, so they stay
at opacity:0 forever. Removed the hook and all .reveal classes entirely.

redesigned ModelTrainingTab with category + per-category dropdown, and wires

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Phase 5: Infrastructure & DevOps — 9 services + Prometheus/Grafana/Jaeger

Observability domain (5 services, ports 8190-8194):
- alerting-service: Alert rules, firing, routing, silencing
- health-check-service: Service probes, dependency graph, dashboard
- metrics-aggregation-service: Counters/gauges/histograms, aggregation
- log-aggregation-service: Structured log ingestion, querying, patterns
- trace-collector-service: Span collection, trace assembly, latency analysis

DevOps domain (4 services, ports 8195-8198):
- deployment-service: Blue-green/canary/rolling, rollback, promote
- slo-tracker-service: SLO definitions, error budgets, burn rate alerts
- synthetic-monitor-service: HTTP/DNS/TCP probes, uptime reports
- chaos-experiment-service: Fault injection, steady-state verification

Infrastructure:
- Prometheus v2.51 (port 9090) with scrape configs for all services
- Grafana v10.4 (port 3333) with auto-provisioned datasources
- Jaeger v1.55 (port 16686) for distributed tracing
- Shared observability library (metrics, tracing, logging, health)
- PostgreSQL devops schema (11 tables, 10 indexes)

K8s/Helm/Terraform:
- K8s manifests: 8 infrastructure deployments + Kustomize overlays (dev/staging/prod)
- Helm chart: mobility-platform with deployment, service, ingress, HPA templates
- Terraform modules: kubernetes-cluster, database, monitoring + environments

Learning modules M25-M30 (302 tests):
- M25 Kubernetes Fundamentals: Pod lifecycle, deployments, services, ingress
- M26 Helm & Kustomize: Template rendering, values merge, overlays, promotion
- M27 CI/CD Pipeline Design: Pipeline DAG, deployment strategies, rollback
- M28 Observability Stack: Metrics types, distributed tracing, log correlation
- M29 Service Mesh: Sidecar proxy, traffic routing, circuit breaker, mTLS
- M30 Infrastructure as Code: Resource graphs, state management, plan/apply

6 exercises: Pod scheduler, Helm renderer, canary deployment, metric aggregator,
circuit breaker state machine, Terraform planner (topological sort)

Total: 132 services (120 prior + 9 new + 3 infra), 452 new tests (150 service + 302 learning)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix CSS class names to match stylesheet

Align all layout and page HTML with the actual class names defined in
assets/css/main.css. The stylesheet uses a flat/hybrid BEM convention
(site-header, site-logo, site-nav, nav-links, section__heading,
hero__headline, service-detail-card, footer-grid, etc.) rather than the
fully-nested BEM names initially used in the templates.

- default.html: use site-header/site-logo/site-nav/nav-links/nav-cta,
  CSS checkbox mobile nav, site-footer/footer-grid/footer-col structure
- post.html: wrap content in post-content div, fix post-meta markup
- index.md: hero__headline, section__label/heading/subheading,
  feature-card__icon, cta-section structure, blog-card__body/date/link
- services.md: service-detail-card and child classes
- contact.md: contact-grid two-col, contact-info__icon/label/value
- blog.md: post-list__date, remove unstyled meta/link classes

feat: non-Preferent stats — season totals, box scores, game log

All three Stats sub-tabs now work for non-Preferent kids (Promoció,
1r Any). SeasonStatsFromLog computes GP/W-L/PPG/VAL/FT%/PF from
cached box scores when msstats team-stats returns empty. Full team
table is a follow-on (~45 min).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update technical overview for non-Preferent stats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle out-of-range RamRa addresses in one-hot sparse commit

Non-memory cycles produce addr=k_chunk (out of range) from
buildRamRaPolynomial. The previous dense code silently skipped these
via `if (addr < k_chunk)`, but the one-hot PR replaced the guard with
`assert(addr < k_chunk)` causing crashes on all programs with RAM access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: enforce minimum bun version (>= 1.3.5) during webmux init

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: enable OCR filter to pull Tesseract libs into avfilter link

subtitle_ocr.o is an unconditional libavfilter object that uses
Tesseract via #if CONFIG_LIBTESSERACT. The Tesseract link flags are
only added to EXTRALIBS-avfilter when a filter declares a dependency
on libtesseract. Enable the OCR filter (which has
ocr_filter_deps="libtesseract") to pull in the required libs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: FFmpeg fork with PGS/subtitle patches. 21 commits on pgs-series branch.

commit.message:
feat: strip buttons and block non-group commands in group chats

Group mode is now minimal: no inline keyboards on cards, no callback
queries, only /set_task /unset_task /task_status commands allowed.
Interaction is purely text-based via @mention and reply.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix duplicate image key bug in listing editor by using unique IDs

Images with the same filename caused React key collisions, leading to
wrong counts, stale DOM nodes, and duplicate cover badges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: move C++ runtime to --extra-libs for correct link order

FFmpeg's configure link test uses: $LDFLAGS ... $TMPO $libs $extralibs
The C++ runtime (-lstdc++/-lc++) must come AFTER libtesseract.a in link
order so GNU ld resolves C++ symbols referenced by the static library.
Move from --extra-ldflags (maps to $LDFLAGS, before libs) to
--extra-libs (maps to $extralibs, after libs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Column layout picker, file drag-drop, social overhaul, sidebar wider

- Klaviyo-style column layout picker dialog (1-4 cols with preset widths)
- Drag & drop image files from desktop onto canvas/columns/sections
- Client-side image compression before upload (max 1200px, JPEG 85%)
- Social links: accordion UI, 12 platforms, icon styles, custom icons
- Sidebar widened 20% (272px -> 326px)
- Canvas right-side padding fix
- Supabase email-assets bucket created

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: one-hot Dory commits with homomorphic hint combining (#16)

* perf: one-hot batch affine additions for Dory commits

Replace dense K×T field element expansion + Pippenger MSM for one-hot
polynomials (38 of 40 committed polys) with sparse index arrays + batch
affine point additions using Montgomery's batch inversion trick.

- Add batchInversion to MontgomeryField (1 inversion + 3(n-1) muls)
- Add batch_affine.zig with batchG1Additions/batchG1AdditionsMulti
- Add commitOneHotWithPool to Dory (direct G1 basis point summing)
- Store onehot_indices ([][]?u8) instead of dense witness_polys for
  sparse polynomials, reducing memory from ~1.2GB to ~2.4MB
- Sparse joint poly construction: O(T) per poly instead of O(K×T)

All 8 programs verified against upstream Jolt verifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: use projective accumulation instead of naive affine adds

Replace batchG1Additions (1 field inversion per add) with projective
accumulation via addAffine (no inversions) + single toAffine at end.
For rows with ~64 points this eliminates ~63 expensive Fermat inversions.

primes_large: 33.8s → 17.4s (was 26.1s before one-hot change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: homomorphic hint combining for Stage 8 Dory opening

Cache G1 row commitments from the initial Dory commit phase and combine
them homomorphically (Σ γ^k · rows_k[i]) using GLV scalar multiplication
instead of recomputing row commitments from scratch via full MSM in Stage 8.

primes_large: 26.1s → 15.7s (40% total speedup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace batch affine with projective accumulation, extract Dory row index building

Remove batch_affine.zig and switch one-hot Dory commits to projective
accumulation. Extract buildOneHotRowIndexSlices for reuse, add
combineRowCommitmentHints for homomorphic hint combining, simplify
one-hot index assertions, add Fp12.isOne(), and add tests for batch
inversion and one-hot commit correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>


---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Capture on_add hook output and send results to user via Telegram

run_on_add now accepts bot and chat_id params. When provided, runs
the hook script asynchronously, captures stdout/stderr, and sends
the output back as a Telegram message. Falls back to fire-and-forget
Popen when bot/chat_id are not provided.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Excel report download and fix compare response field names

- New GET /projects/{id}/reports/download returns .xlsx with 5 sheets:
  Summary, Matched, Missing in EPLAN, Missing in TIA, Comment Mismatches
- Fix compare response: rename counts->summary, matched_preview->matched,
  comment_mismatch_preview->comment_mismatches; remove preview limits so
  all rows are returned and frontend types match
- Add openpyxl to requirements.txt
- Frontend: Download Excel Report button appears after compare runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resilient embedding job — guaranteed self-chain, throttle handling, per-product recovery

Root causes of stale jobs identified and fixed:

1. getTextEmbedding() now retries on ThrottlingException with backoff
   and returns null instead of throwing (prevents batch-killing errors)
2. computeEmbeddings() uses Promise.allSettled — one product failure
   no longer crashes entire batch; failed products auto-retry next batch
3. Self-chain is GUARANTEED via inner try/catch around loop — after()
   fires even after errors (except user stop/pause or job completion)
4. Pipeline fetchNextBatch wrapped in try/catch inside Promise.all
5. Self-chain fetch has 15s AbortController timeout
6. EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: 40→20 (80→40 concurrent Bedrock calls,
   staying within AWS 50 TPS limit)
7. Batch sizes: 40→20 (images), 60→40 (text-only)
8. Heartbeat TTL: 90s→180s (synced in stats endpoint)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: link C++ runtime for Tesseract static linking on all targets

Tesseract is a C++ library with a C API (capi.h). When FFmpeg's
configure tests linking via the C compiler, C++ symbols from
libtesseract.a are unresolved unless the C++ runtime is explicitly
linked. Add -lstdc++ (Linux/mingw) or -lc++ (macOS/llvm-mingw)
to extra-ldflags on all platforms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
release: v0.14.5 — surface Parquet restore errors on startup (closes #101)

- Replace silent let-chain that swallowed Parquet load errors with explicit
  match that logs tracing::error! when load_all_events() fails
- Previously, corrupted or unreadable Parquet files silently resulted in
  0 events with no error message — identical to "no data on disk"
- Add test verifying error path is exercised on corrupt Parquet files
- Bump all service versions to 0.14.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add filter flags to list and name-based get for key resources

virtual-machines list: --name, --site, --role, --cluster, --status, --tag
virtual-machines get:  --name as alternative to --id (errors if ambiguous)
ip-addresses list:     --virtual-machine, --device, --address

Filters map directly to NetBox API query parameters, so only matching
records are returned server-side rather than fetching the full dataset.

commit.message:
feat: rules/privacy/terms pages + help center + admin content manager

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: season totals for non-Preferent players computed from box scores

msstats team-stats endpoint returns {} for non-Preferent teams.
Rather than showing no season tab, compute season totals from the
per-game box score data already fetched via player-log.

SeasonStatsFromLog aggregates GP, W/L record, PPG, VAL (PIR avg),
FT%, and PF from all available box scores. Renders with same visual
style as SeasonStats. player-log now returns val (valoration) per game.

Flow: statsConfirmed=null → SeasonStats probes msstats (spinner).
If confirmed=true → SeasonStats (full team table from msstats).
If confirmed=false → SeasonStatsFromLog (player averages from log).
Box Scores and Game Log unlock once confirmed resolves either way.

commit.message:
Fix: utöka SKATTETABELL_31 till 200 000 kr (var 120 000 kr)

Lägger till 8 nya datapunkter (125k–200k). Värdet för 135k är
verifierat mot SKV:s kalkylator (netto 82 298 kr). Värden för
125k och 130k är korrigerade från användarens uppskattning (de var
icke-monotona) via linjär interpolation med ~35,57 % marginalskatt.

Uppdaterar även testets lön från 150k (nu i tabellen) till 250k
för att fortsatt testa extrapolation utanför tabellens övre gräns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove custom log/warn/error from runScript, use console.log directly

Removes the dedup logging system (LogEntry, captured log functions, flush
timers) from the runner and the log/warn/error fields from ScriptContext.
Scripts now use standard console.log/warn/error instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
